title: Consumer Stress Spike From Google Trends Search For Rent Increase Notice
thesis: Spike in rent increase searches indicates rising consumer cost pressure and potential discretionary spending cutbacks. Higher housing costs reduce disposable income for discretionary purchases.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'rent increase notice'
signal_logic: If weekly search interest rises by 15% over prior 2 weeks
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search interest rises by 15% over prior 2 weeks Exit: After 4 weeks or if search interest falls below 5% gain
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'rent increase notice' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rent-related stress is common and tends to surge around lease renewal seasons.

schema.idea: Consumer stress spike from Google Trends search for 'rent increase notice'

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'rent increase notice'

schema.behavior: Spike in rent increase searches indicates rising consumer cost pressure and potential discretionary spending cutbacks.

market_impact.rationale: Higher housing costs reduce disposable income for discretionary purchases.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search interest rises by 15% over prior 2 weeks
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if search interest falls below 5% gain

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rent-related stress is common and tends to surge around lease renewal seasons.

title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends Search For Job Quitting Signals Labor Market Stress

data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'job quitting'
signal_logic: If weekly search interest increases by 20% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search interest increases by 20% week-over-week Exit: After 3 weeks or if interest declines by 10% week-over-week

risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'job quitting' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor unrest and quitting are common and often spike seasonally or with economic changes.

title: Weekly Jump In Political Insider Filing Selling By Energy Sector Insiders

entry_exit: Entry: If insider selling volume exceeds 150% of 4-week average Exit: After 3 weeks or if selling volume returns below 110% of average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Insider selling activity fluctuates frequently with earnings and regulatory cycles.

title: Daily Jump In Open Ev Charger Map Counts In A Metro Area Indicating Accelerated Ev Adoption
thesis: Spike in new EV chargers suggests accelerated local EV infrastructure deployment. Consumer discretionary EV-related firms benefit from infrastructure expansion.

signal_logic: If daily new charger count in metro increases 40% above 7-day average
entry_exit: Entry: If daily new charger count in metro increases 40% above 7-day average Exit: After 7 trading days or if count falls below 10% above average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap daily new EV charger counts by metro area via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV infrastructure expansions happen frequently in waves and can be detected daily.
